---
title: "About"
toc: false
---

<p class="about-intro">
I came to physics by an indirect path. I began in life sciences at Université de Strasbourg in 2018 and spent two years there before switching to a preparatory class (MPSI) at Lycée privé ORT in Strasbourg in 2020. I completed a Bachelor's in Physics at Université de Strasbourg (2022 – 2025), and am now in the first year of a master's in fundamental physics with a cosmology specialisation at Université Claude Bernard Lyon 1.
</p>

## Research interests

My interests sit at the interface of cosmology and particle physics. I am drawn most strongly to:

- **Neutrino cosmology** : how neutrino properties (masses, hierarchy, thermal history) leave their imprint on large-scale structure and the cosmic microwave background
- **Astroparticle physics** : from underwater Cherenkov telescopes to gravitational-wave observatories
- **The mathematical edge of physics** : the places where the formalism is doing more work than the intuition

## Training & background

In the spring of 2024 I spent a research internship at IPHC Strasbourg with the [KM3NeT](https://www.km3net.org/) collaboration, building Monte Carlo tools to simulate the detector response in Python and C++. The work was independent and deposited on GitHub.

Before Lyon, I prepared at Lycée privé ORT in Strasbourg (MPSI, 2020 – 2022) and then completed my Bachelor's in Physics at Université de Strasbourg (2022 – 2025), with optional courses in astrophysics in the second year and on the formation and evolution of galaxies in the third year. I am a member of the [Société Française de Physique](https://www.sfpnet.fr/).
